goal: Implement Aether Skills Layer — smart-matched colony and domain skills for workers

phases.name: Skill Engine Foundation
phases.description: Create skills.sh utility with frontmatter parsing, index caching, codebase detection, skill matching, and injection. Wire into aether-utils.sh dispatch. Write and pass all tests.

tasks.goal: Write failing bash tests for skill frontmatter parsing, index building, caching, role-based matching, role filtering, skill injection, and skill listing
constraints: Tests create isolated temp dirs with sample SKILL.md files
success_criteria: All tests fail because subcommands don't exist yet

tasks.goal: Create .aether/utils/skills.sh with all core functions: frontmatter parser, index builder, cached index reader, codebase detector, skill matcher, skill injector, skill lister, manifest reader, skill diff
constraints: Use process substitution (not pipe-to-while) to avoid subshell variable loss
Use find for recursive file detection (not compgen -G with **)
Log trimmed skills to stderr when budget exceeded
hints: Implementation plan has complete code at docs/plans/2026-03-22-aether-skills-layer.md

tasks.goal: Wire skills.sh into aether-utils.sh: add source line, add 10 dispatch cases, add Skills section to help
constraints: Use pattern matching to find insertion points
hints: Search for 'midden' in dispatch table to find insertion area

tasks.goal: Create AVA unit tests for skill subcommands and add test:skills npm script to package.json
constraints: Use execSync to call aether-utils.sh (follow existing AVA patterns)
Add test:skills script to package.json scripts
hints: npm run test:bash only runs test-aether-utils.sh — new test files need their own npm script
success_criteria: npm run test:skills runs bash tests

tasks.goal: Run all tests and verify everything passes
success_criteria: npm run test:unit passes including skills tests
skills.sh exists with all core functions
All bash and AVA tests pass

phases.name: Skill Content Authoring
phases.description: Create all 10 colony SKILL.md files, all 18 domain SKILL.md files, both manifests, and domain README. Verify full library indexes correctly.

tasks.goal: Create 10 colony skill directories and SKILL.md files under .aether/skills/colony/
constraints: Body teaches the process guide from spec
hints: Skills: colony-interaction, colony-visuals, pheromone-visibility, build-discipline, colony-lifecycle, context-management, state-safety, error-presentation, pheromone-protocol, worker-priming

tasks.goal: Create 18 domain skill directories and SKILL.md files under .aether/skills/domain/
constraints: Frontmatter has detect_files and detect_packages matching spec Section 6
hints: Frontend: react, nextjs, vue, tailwind, html-css, svelte; Backend: nodejs, python, django, rails, golang; Data: postgresql, rest-api, graphql, prisma; Infra: docker, typescript, testing

tasks.goal: Create both .manifest.json files and domain README.md

phases.name: Build Pipeline Integration
phases.description: Wire skill detection and per-worker injection into build-context.md, build-wave.md, AND build-verify.md so both builders and watchers receive matched skills.

tasks.goal: Add skill detection step to build-context.md (index + detect, store results as cross-stage state)
constraints: Display indexed and matched counts to user
hints: Plan Task 5 Step 1 has the markdown

tasks.goal: Add per-worker skill injection to build-wave.md (match by builder role + task, append skill_section after prompt_section)
constraints: Display colony + domain skill counts per worker
hints: Plan Task 5 Step 2 has the markdown

tasks.goal: Add skill injection to build-verify.md watcher prompt (same mechanism as build-wave.md but for watcher role)
constraints: Watcher prompt already has { prompt_section } — add { skill_section } alongside it
Match skills for watcher role, not builder
hints: build-verify.md line 27 has the existing prompt_section injection point

success_criteria: Skill detection runs once per build
Autopilot (/ant:run) gets skills automatically via same playbooks

phases.name: Hub Distribution and Skill Creation Command
phases.description: Add manifest-aware skills sync to setupHub, create /ant:skill-create command with OpenCode parity stub.

tasks.goal: Add skills directory sync to setupHub in bin/cli.js — copy managed skills to ~/.aether/skills/ while preserving user-created skills
constraints: Never modify or delete user-created skills
Log notice when user skill conflicts with shipped name
hints: Plan Task 6 has the setupHub code
success_criteria: After npm install -g, ~/.aether/skills/ has 28 skill directories

tasks.goal: Create /ant:skill-create slash command for Claude Code with Oracle research wizard
hints: Spec Section 7 has the complete flow
success_criteria: Command file exists and follows wizard flow

tasks.goal: Create matching OpenCode command stub at .opencode/commands/ant/skill-create.md for lint:sync parity
constraints: CRITICAL: filename must match exactly or lint:sync fails (exit 1)
Content adapted for OpenCode (uses normalize-args pattern)
success_criteria: npm run lint:sync passes (command counts match)

tasks.goal: Run npm run lint:sync to verify command parity

success_criteria: setupHub installs skills with manifest protection
skill-create command exists in both Claude and OpenCode

phases.name: Documentation and Validation
phases.description: Update CLAUDE.md and aether-colony.md, run full test suite, linters, package validation, and end-to-end skill flow.

tasks.goal: Add Skills System section to CLAUDE.md and update Quick Reference table
Add /ant:skill-create to aether-colony.md Available Commands table
Run full test suite, linters, and package validation
constraints: All 530+ existing tests must still pass
npm run lint must pass including lint:sync

tasks.goal: Run end-to-end skill flow: index → detect → match → inject and verify non-empty skill_section
hints: Plan Task 10 Step 4 has the commands
success_criteria: skill-match returns at least 1 colony skill

learnings.claim: Process substitution (< <(cmd)) avoids subshell variable loss in while loops piped from jq — critical for accumulating results in bash
learnings.evidence: 19 bash tests pass including scoring loops that modify variables inside while loops

learnings.claim: TDD with background agents works well — write tests first in one agent, implementation in parallel agent, then merge results
learnings.evidence: Forge-1 wrote 9 failing tests while Anvil-2 built skills.sh; all tests passed on first wire-up

learnings.claim: Parallel agents for content authoring (colony vs domain) is highly efficient — independent file sets with no conflicts
learnings.evidence: 28 SKILL.md files created by 2 parallel builders with zero file conflicts

learnings.claim: Playbook modifications (markdown instruction files) can safely be parallelized when each agent edits a different file — no merge conflicts
learnings.evidence: 3 agents edited build-context.md, build-wave.md, and build-verify.md simultaneously with zero conflicts

decisions: Skills inject outside colony-prime as separate skill_section with 12K budget
OpenCode command parity required for lint:sync (hard gate)
New bash tests need explicit npm script (test:skills) — not auto-discovered
Watcher prompt in build-verify.md also needs skill injection
Autopilot uses same playbooks — no separate code path to maintain

instincts.trigger: building bash utilities with scoring/accumulation loops
instincts.action: use process substitution (< <(jq)) not pipes to while loops — pipes create subshells that lose variable modifications
evidence: 19 tests validated scoring functions work correctly with process substitution
